description: Bees turn gathered nectar or similar plant secretions into 'hemilo', a sweet, concentrated substance stored in the hive. It is food made through the work of a colony. Syrups and other sweet viscous substances may resemble honey without becoming honey.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced breath pass through the open glottis for 'h' into mid-front 'e'. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', release into stressed high-front 'i', then touch the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and finish on rounded mid-back 'o'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Honey carries the season, forage, weather, and health of a colony into one substance. Good keeping follows those relations before it asks what can be harvested.

semantic_domains.nature: a substance produced by bees from gathered plant material
semantic_domains.physical: a sweet viscous food with a distinct material identity
